item.title: Takamine GB72CE-BSB G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Brown Sunburst Finish — $899
item.description:
$899 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Takamine GB72CE-BSB G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Brown Sunburst Finish

The GB72CE-BSB sits comfortably in hand with a perfectly balanced neck profile and smooth rosewood fretboard that invites effortless playing across all registers. The jumbo body delivers a warm, woody tone with impressive clarity and articulation, giving your notes definition without harshness. This is an instrument that rewards both meticulous technique and raw feel, offering a genuine acoustic experience that electric basses simply cannot replicate.

Playability & Build

Constructed with a solid spruce top and flame maple back and sides, this bass features a comfortable mahogany neck with a 16″ radius and 21 frets across a 34″ scale length. The 1.77″ synthetic bone nut and rosewood bridge with compensated synthetic bone saddle ensure reliable intonation and stable tuning across the instrument's frequency range.

Tone & Electronics

The onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp captures both the subtle nuances of fingerpicked passages and the punchy attack of picked lines with authentic acoustic character. A full 3-band EQ with bypass, gain and mid-shift controls, and a dedicated bass-boost switch provide comprehensive shaping of your tone, while the integrated chromatic tuner keeps you locked in during performance and setup.

Key Features
  • 4-string jumbo body with brown sunburst gloss finish
  • Solid spruce top with flame maple back and sides
  • Mahogany neck with 16″ radius
  • Rosewood fingerboard and bridge with compensated synthetic bone saddle
  • 34″ scale length with 21 frets
  • Synthetic bone nut (1.77″ width)
  • Chrome die-cast tuners
  • Takamine TK-40B preamp with onboard chromatic tuner
  • 3-band EQ with bypass, gain, mid-shift, and bass-boost controls
  • Pre-strung with D'Addario EXP PBB170 coated phosphor bronze bass strings (.045–.100)

The GB72CE-BSB delivers responsive, natural amplification that preserves the acoustic character and tonal complexity of its construction, making it an ideal choice for players seeking a professional-grade acoustic bass.

item.title: Takamine GB30CE-BLK G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Black Finish — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Takamine GB30CE-BLK G-Series Acoustic Electric Bass in Black Finish

The GB30CE-BLK is a professional-grade acoustic-electric bass that balances playability with tonal sophistication. Its Jumbo Cutaway body delivers commanding low-end presence while the Venetian-style cutaway provides comfortable upper-fret access. This is a serious instrument built to perform consistently in studio sessions, live settings, and practice environments alike.

Construction & Playability

Built with a solid Spruce top paired with Mahogany back and sides, this bass produces a warm, balanced tone with natural sustain. The Mahogany neck with Laurel fingerboard offers excellent durability and a smooth playing surface, while the Dovetail neck joint ensures stability and resonance transfer. At 864mm scale length with a 1.77" nut width, the instrument maintains proper string spacing for precise intonation and comfortable fingering technique across the fretboard.

Electronics & Amplification

The integrated Takamine TK-40B preamp provides comprehensive tone-shaping capabilities with a built-in tuner, three-band EQ, gain controls, mid-shift control, and bass-boost switch—giving you studio-grade control in any setting. The EQ bypass function lets you switch between processed and unprocessed tones instantly, while Synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle preserve acoustic resonance and clarity through the amplification chain.

Key Features
  • Jumbo Cutaway body with Venetian-style cutaway for upper-fret access
  • Solid Spruce top with Mahogany back and sides
  • Mahogany neck with Dovetail joint construction
  • Laurel fingerboard with Synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle
  • 864mm scale length with 1.77" nut width
  • 4-String configuration
  • Takamine TK-40B preamp with built-in tuner and three-band EQ
  • Mid-shift control, gain controls, bass-boost switch, and EQ bypass
  • Chrome die-cast tuners
  • Gloss finish in Black
  • Dimensions: 1214.7mm length × 435mm width × 125mm depth

The GB30CE-BLK represents exceptional value for bassists seeking premium Takamine craftsmanship at a used-market price point.

item.title: Schecter Hellraiser Extreme-5 Left-Handed Electric Bass Crimson — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Hellraiser Extreme-5 Left-Handed Electric Bass in Crimson Red Burst Satin

The Hellraiser Extreme-5 LH delivers a lightweight, ergonomic neck-through design that sits comfortably in extended playing sessions, with a thin 'C' profile neck that accommodates both aggressive technique and precise fingerwork. The 35-inch scale and ultra-access body cutaway make this left-handed instrument equally suited to studio work and live performance. Finished in striking Crimson Red Burst Satin with black chrome hardware, this bass combines aggressive aesthetics with refined playability.

Playability & Construction

Built with a neck-through mahogany body topped in quilted maple, the Hellraiser Extreme-5 LH achieves both structural integrity and visual impact. The Maple/Walnut multi-ply neck paired with an ebony fretboard and 24 X-Jumbo frets on a 16-inch radius provides fast response and consistent intonation across the full range. The thin 'C' neck profile—measuring .787" at the first fret and .866" at the 12th—allows for comfortable thumb positioning and efficient hand transitions without sacrificing stability.

Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG 40TW pickups deliver articulate, punchy tone with exceptional clarity across the five-string spectrum, powered by an 18-volt battery system for enhanced headroom and dynamic range. The Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut and Schecter custom bass bridge coupled with Grover tuners ensure reliable tuning stability and optimal sustain, while the MOP binding with multi-ply detailing and MOP Gothic cross inlays add distinctive visual character.

Key Features
  • Neck-through construction with ultra access body cutaway
  • Mahogany body with quilted maple top
  • Maple/walnut multi-ply neck with thin 'C' profile
  • Ebony fretboard with 24 X-Jumbo frets and MOP Gothic cross inlays
  • 35-inch scale length (889mm) with 16-inch fretboard radius
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 1.771" width
  • Dual EMG 40TW active pickups with 18-volt battery system
  • Grover tuners with 2-way adjustable truss rod
  • Schecter custom bass bridge with string-through design
  • Black chrome hardware throughout
  • MOP binding with black/white/black multi-ply detail
  • Left-handed configuration with factory setup and full warranty

This combination of active electronics, premium hardware, and expert construction makes the Hellraiser Extreme-5 LH a serious instrument for left-handed bassists seeking professional-grade performance and distinctive visual presence.

item.title: Bogart Blackstone bass — $2,328.60
item.description:
$2,328.60 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

A real high quality, handmade bass from the German brand Bogart. Chock-full of innovative technology. The body is made from a composite material and the neck is a SKC Vacuum Carbone bolt-on neck with a 24 fret phenolic fretboard and zero fret. Those aren't tuners on the headstocks but string clamps. The system is designed to use normal (one ball end) bass strings and to make string changing more convenient,It comes with a set of Bartolini J style pickups and active 2-way EQ electronics. The bass is in really good condition and is super easy to play.

Weight: 4.15kg

item.title: Fender Aerodyne Jazz Bass - Dolphin Grey (metallic) — $1,283.71
item.description:
$1,283.71 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

Just a fantastic bass! Light weight,3.66kg, super nice and slim neck and a P & J pick up configuration. Absolute joy to play and a shop favorite. It has some nicks and dings and the frets show some standard wear but nothing major. It has a radiused, carved basswood body with cream binding in a stunning metallic Dolphin Grey finish, with matching headstock. And boy, are we suckers for matching headstocks! The neck is maple, with a “Slim C” neck profile and a rosewood fretboard. This bass was introduced in 2006, as a Japanese non export model.

item.title: Lestrange Longhorn Bass - Sunburst — $954.13
item.description:
$954.13 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

A very cool parts bass from Sweden's Lestrange. This 30” short scale, hollow body bass, is comprised of a new old stock 1960’s semi hollow body from Kawai. The neck comes from a 70’s Greco violin bass. The pickups are Kingston and the tail piece comes from a Framus Rio. The knobs are repro Harmony bakelite. This bass is an absolute delight to play, slick action and a really nice slim neck and the weight is just 2.75kg

item.title: Aria Jazz Bass 1540C 1976 — $567.22
item.description:
$567.22 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

Aria Jazz Bass model 1540C neck and parts from 1976 on a recent Candy Apple red body with new pickguard. It’s Incredibly light weight and comfortable. The original '76 maple neck has a beautiful rosewood fretboard with block inlays and bindings. The neck has more of a Precision bass feel, has a recent refret and the frets show no signs of wear. The body has some nicks and dings but nothing major. Weight: 3.6kg

item.title: Aria SB-600 1980 - Natural — $1,003.09
item.description:
$1,003.09 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

The Aria Pro II SB-600 bass guitar was, as with all Aria electrics up until 1987, produced in Japan by premier producer Matsumoku. It was part of the "Super Bass" (SB) series. The SB-600 has a set neck construction, brass bridge and nut, It features passive electronics, with a single double-coil pickup, that you can split with a coil-tap switch.The bass is in great shape for a bass from 1980 that has been played just minor nicks and dings so it’s been taken good care of. Frets show some wear but nothing major. The bass plays great and packs a real punch and is a great alternative for those seeking the SB 1000 vibe without the now hefty price tags. As can be seen in a pic, one tuner is missing its back plate but functions as it should. A replacement is ordered but the price atm is for the bass in its present condition, without replaced tuner. Weight: 4.53kg Scale 34”

item.title: Kramer DMZ4001 Early 80’s — $1,862.88
item.description:
$1,862.88 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

A very cool Alu necked Kramer DMZ4001 in really good condition just taken out of our collection. Very good condition w/ only minor dings. Plays and sounds great. Frets looks terrific. Comes with its original tan Kramer hardcase, also in very good condition. The DMZ denotation stands for the DiMarzio factory installed pickup.

Aluminum & walnut neck with ebonol fingerboard & dot inlays. All original with its Schaller tuners. 34" scale length. Solid walnut body with maple center strips. DiMarzio split single coil with master volume & tone controls. Two factory mini toggle for different wirings and sound.

item.title: Kramer DMZ 5000 1979-1982 — $1,665.84
item.description:
$1,665.84 · Very Good · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

The Kramer DMZ 5000 is a notable and top of the line model from the "aluminum neck era" of the late 1970s and early 1980s. It was part of Kramer's second major series of instruments, all featuring the iconic "tuning fork" headstock design, DiMarzio pickups(hence DMZ) and a unique hybrid construction. 

In very good condition for its age, just some nicks and dings and a missing neck bolt cover plate. Frets looks good and have plenty of meat on them. Easy to play. 

Neck Design: A bolt-on aluminum T-beam neck with walnut  inserts on the back to provide a more traditional wood feel for the thumb.

Fretboard: Rosewood fretboard, likely installed in favour of the serie's original synthetic ebonol board.

Body Construction: Features a solid, double-cutaway body, made of walnut with maple center stripes

Electronics: Equipped with factory DiMarzio pickups in a P/J configuration 

Controls: Passive electronics including two volumes, two tones, a 3-way pickup selector, and two mini-toggle switches for coil-tapping or frequency shifting.

item.title: Squier Precision Bass silver series 1993-94 UNPLAYED — $1,504.63
item.description:
$1,504.63 · Excellent · Twang Vintage Gear Emporium

This is a true collector's item from a pivotal period in Fender's history, albeit under the Squier brand! Imagine, this is a 30+ year old bass in mint, unplayed condition, still in its original box with matching serial number stamped on the label! Plastic still on the pickguard! You will be hard pressed to ever find one again in the same condition and this a definite conversational piece.

When Fender ceased production of Squier guitars in Korea and India in the early 90s and returned to Japan and the Fuji-Gen Factory, they went out of their way to through quality quench the competition with its first upgraded lineup. The silver series got its name from the silver transitional Fender logo on the headstocks. After word got out that these instruments were really good, Fender added the Silver Series name to the logos. This one has a nice thin neck, feels more like a Jazz Bass in the neck profile, perhaps just a tad bit wider. It’s super resonant and responsive. Straight out of the box, without any set up, it plays great! Feels like the neck and body are two pieces of really good wood, that works great together. Weight: 3.94kg

item.title: Moon JJ-4 2010-2020 - Transparent black — $1,671.81
item.description:
$1,671.81 · Very Good · Musical Trades

Now available, this Moon JJ-4, an iconic model from the Japanese brand manufactured by PGM. Known in North America thanks to artists such as Larry Graham and Prince, and especially in Japan thanks to virtuoso Mitsuru Sutoh, this bass excels at slap playing and groove. This is a model with active electronics, featuring Bartolini pickups and a Bartolini TBT preamp with 2-band EQ. The controls are extremely effective, inherited from the Jazz Bass but more modern: neck volume, bridge volume, bass, treble. The weight is more than reasonable, and the finish is well made but without frills. Unlike models from the 1980s and 1990s, this one is lightweight, weighing around 4 kg, and very comfortable, with good balance when playing standing up or sitting down. Condition: Very good with a few dents on the neck and headstock that have no impact on the structure. A chip in the finish at the bottom of the body where the electronics plate meets the body has been touched up by luthier Régis Sala (Salaguitars.com) and is no longer visible. Delivered with semi-rigid carry bag.

Shipping includes comprehensive insurance coverage, regardless of the service used.

Shipping prices outside Europe are generic only; please feel free to request a personalized quote.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

item.title: Moon JJ-4 2010-2020 Translucent blue — $1,910.64
item.description:
$1,910.64 · Very Good · Musical Trades

This is another awesome made in Japan bass, the moon JJ-4. Well known in NOrth America, Europe and mosltky Japan, Moon is one of the most reputable bass brands in Japan. Artists such as Larry Graham, Prince, and Mitsuru Sutoh have popularised these instruments.

This is an active electronics model constructed from ash and maple for the neck, with a very fast ebony fingerboard with it's distinctive sound and feel, equipped with hand-wired Moon pickups and a Bartolini TBT preamp with 2-band EQ. The controls are extremely effective, inherited from the Jazz Bass in a more modern design: neck volume, bridge volume, bass, treble; so what else is needed?

Unlike models from the 1980s and 1990s, this one is lightweight, weighing around 4 kg, extremely comfortable, with good balance when playing standing up or sitting down. The finish is well made but without frills. Unlike the models from the 80s and 90s, this one is light, weighing around 4 kg, and very comfortable, well balanced for playing standing up or sitting down. Condition: Very good with a few dents on the neck and headstock that have no impact on the structure. A chip in the finish at the bottom of the body where the electronics plate meets the body has been touched up by luthier Régis Sala (Salaguitars.com) and is no longer visible. Comes with a new Moon genuine gig bag .

item.title: Moon DJB-5 2020-2025 - Transparent brown burst — $2,448.01
item.description:
$2,448.01 · Excellent · Musical Trades

Vend basse Moon fabriquée par PGM au Japon. La marque y est reconnue par les professionnels ainsi qu’en Amérique du Nord et en Europe. Corps en frêne et manche érable, touche ébène. Filets du corps en marqueterie rapportés au corps, avec un léger arrondi ergonomique.
Diapason 34’ avec un Si grave très pêchu, une réponse et attaque étonnantes. Micros de type SoapBar avec réglages Volume Volume, et 2EQ basses et aigus, extrêmement efficace. Les deux micros en phase donne un son de slap assez très impressionnant. Poids de l’ordre de 4.4 kg, avec un très bon équilibre: l’instrument ne fatigue pas, même sur une session prolongée en studio. Livrée avec housse d’origine, très solide. Ensemble de fabrication et finition remarquables, a découvrir.

Up for sale, a Moon bass manufactured by PGM in Japan. The brand is recognised by professionals in Japan, North America and Europe. Ash body and maple neck, ebony fingerboard. Inlaid body binding with a slight ergonomic curve.

34" scale length with a very punchy low B, amazing response and attack. SoapBar pickups with volume and 2EQ bass and treble controls, extremely effective. The two pickups in phase give a very impressive slap sound. Weight around 4.4 kg, with very good balance: the instrument does not cause fatigue, even during extended studio sessions. Comes with original, very sturdy cover.

Outstanding craftsmanship and finish ant tone, well worth discovering.

item.title: Lakland USA 44-94 2002 - Amber — $3,331.69
item.description:
$3,331.69 · Excellent · Musical Trades

A vendre cette très belle Lakland USA de 2002 de couleur ambre transparent. Un manche très fin et d’un profil très agréable, poids de moins de 4 kg. Micros Bartolini de type Jazz /Humbucker avec inverseur de phase. Préampli Bartolini avec réglages 3EQ, coupure sur le potard de volume et balance. Deux vis du capot électronique manquantes ont été remplacées l’identique des vis originales qui se fixent dans des inserts en cuivre fixés sur le corps typiques des Lakland anciennes. Jeu extrêmement rapide et souple. Livrée avec son étui original G&G deluxe beige en parfait état.

English:Up For sale: this beautiful 2002 Lakland USA in transparent amber. A very thin neck with a very comfortable profile, weighing less than 4 kg. Bartolini Jazz/Humbucker pickups with phase inverter. Bartolini preamp with 3EQ controls, cut-off on the volume knob and balance. Two missing screws on the electronics cover have been replaced with identical original screws that fit copper inserts fixed to the body like the 90-2000’s Lakland guitars. A real player bass for all styles of music. Comes with its original G&G deluxe beige case in excellent condition.

item.title: Lakland 55-94 USA 2003 - Amber Quilt — $4,179.54
item.description:

Up for sale an awesome bass to play whatever the syle you like. The bass is made from the Lakland facility in Chicago. Comes with factory custom Seymour Duncan Bassline Jazz / Humbucker pickups. Excellent condition, only a little chip close to the jack ouput socket. Comes with its original Lakland US beige shell case.

Bought this new for a project that never happened. Basically mint. Played 3 or four times. Great neck, easy player. Huge range of sounds with the six position switch. Iconic. No dings or scratches

item.title: Used Ernie Ball Musicman Dark Ray 5 - Starry Night w/Case — $2,599.99
item.description:
$2,599.99 · Very Good · ProAudioStar

Designed in collaboration with world-renowned bass accessory manufacturer Darkglass Electronics, the DarkRay unlocks a whole new range of sonic capabilities.

At the heart of every instrument is its tone: in this case, a modern 2 Band EQ preamp was designed by Darkglass specifically for the DarkRay. This preamp offers three modes with very distinct and useful sounds: Clean, Alpha (distortion), and Omega (fuzz). Both distortion circuits are fully mixable via an onboard gain knob and blend control manipulated to the user’s preference. Astonishing clean, slight gain, or a brutal fuzz tone is obtainable, all with no low-end loss. A bright LED ring and the 3-way lever switch indicates which mode the bass is in.

The DarkRay is every bit a Stingray Special with neodymium humbucking pickup, roasted maple neck, comfort contoured body, 22 stainless steel frets, and the familiar 4+1 headstock configuration.

Roasted Neck

The Darkray Features A Roasted Maple Neck, Ebony Fingerboard With Stainless Steel Frets, and Matte Black Hardware.

Darkray 5 Preamp

The 2-Band Preamp Includes Volume, Treble, And Bass Controls With A Separate Blend And Gain Knob To Achieve The Desired Tone and Output.

Roasted Neck

The Darkray 5 Features A Roasted Maple Neck, Ebony Fingerboard With Stainless Steel Frets, And Matte Black Hardware.

Stingray Meets Darkglass

CUSTOM DARKGLASS CLEAN 2-BAND ACTIVE PREAMP

The DarkRay is equipped with a new modern 2 Band preamp that Darkglass specifically designed. The preamp is extremely clean and quiet with adjustable volume, gain, bass, treble, and blend controls to achieve the desired tone.

Alpha and Omega

The Darkglass preamp offers two highly regarded drive circuits, Alpha(tight distortion) and Omega(fuzz). Both distortion circuits are fully mixable via an onboard gain knob and blend control manipulated to the user’s preference with no low-end loss. Via the 3-way lever switch, a bright color-coded LED ring is illuminated, signifying which mode the bass is in.

Darkray = Stingray

The DarkRay bass is every bit a Stingray Special with neodymium humbucking pickup, roasted maple neck, comfort contoured body, 22 stainless steel frets, all black hardware, and the familiar 3+1 headstock configuration.

Features

  • Color : Starry Night
  • Neck : Roasted Maple w/ Ebony Fretboard
  • Pickguard : Black
  • Hardware : Black
  • Size : 13-3/8" wide, 1-5/8" thick, 45-3/4" long (34.0 cm wide, 4.1 cm thick, 116.2 cm long)
  • Body Wood : Select Hardwood
  • Body Finish : High Gloss Polyester
  • Bridge : Vintage Music Man top loaded Black plated, steel bridge plate with vintage Black plated steel saddles
  • Pickguard : Black
  • Scale Length : 34"
  • Neck Radius : 11" (27.9 cm)
  • Headstock Size : Only 8-3/4" (22.2 cm) long
  • Frets : 22 - High profile, wide, stainless steel
  • Neck Width : 1-3/4" (44.5 mm) at the nut; 2-3/4" (69.9 mm) at the last fret
  • Neck Wood : Roasted Maple
  • Fingerboard : Ebony
  • Fret Markers : Dot
  • Neck Finish : Gunstock oil and hand-rubbed special wax blend
  • Neck Colors : Natural
  • Tuning Machines : Custom Music Man, lightweight with tapered string posts and ergonomic clover design
  • Neck Attachment : 5 bolts - perfect alignment with no shifting; Sculpted neck joint allows smooth access to higher frets
  • Electronic Shielding : Graphite acrylic resin coated body cavity and aluminum lined pickguard
  • Controls : vol, gain, blend, treble, bass
  • Switching : 3-way lever distortion selector; Preamp Only, Preamp+Alpha Distortion, Preamp+Omega Distortion
  • Pickups : Single Humbucking with Neodymium magnets
  • Strings : 45w-65w-80w-100w-130w (Regular Slinky Bass #2836)
  • Case Included

item.title: Ibanez SR800-GN Standard Soundgear Bass 1999 - 2003 - Gray Nickel — $625.42
item.description:
$625.42 · Good · James's Gear Outlet

1999 Ibanez SR800 Soundgear Gray Nickel - MIJ FujiGen - Excellent Player!

Up for sale is a cool and rare 1999 Ibanez SR800 in Gray Nickel finish. This is a genuine Japanese-made (FujiGen) example from the golden era of Ibanez Soundgear basses. Serial number starts with F99, confirming 1999 production.

This SR800 features the classic offset double-cutaway basswood body, fast bolt-on maple neck with rosewood fingerboard (24 frets, pearl dot inlays), Ibanez AFR active pickups, and the versatile Vari-Mid 3-band EQ. It plays beautifully with low action, great fretwork, and that signature SR comfort and punchy tone.

Condition notes:

This bass is in very good condition for its 27-year age and shows normal signs of play. There are various small paint chips and dings consistent with normal use—mostly light edge wear on the body and a couple of small chips/scuffs on the back near the electronics cavity. The sparkly Gray Nickel finish still pops and looks killer under stage lights. One of the stacked control knobs is split/cracked (common on these older pots) but functions perfectly with no issues. Electronics are fully working, neck is straight, and it stays in tune well. Overall a solid, gig-ready instrument with plenty of life left.

Comes with a basic gig bag (or soft case if applicable—please confirm).

These MIJ SR800s are becoming harder to find, especially in this metallic finish. Fantastic bass for rock, metal, funk, or studio work—super versatile and comfortable.

Specs:

•  Year: 1999

•  Made in Japan (FujiGen)

•  Model: SR800 Gray Nickel

•  Neck: Maple, bolt-on

•  Fingerboard: Rosewood, 24 frets

•  Pickups: Ibanez AFR active

•  Electronics: Active 3-band EQ with Vari-Mid

•  Weight: [add if you weigh it]

•  Serial: F9933784

Feel free to message with any questions or for more photos

Ships from Canada. 

Will be packed securely with proper protection. 

Buyer responsible for any applicable duties or import fees.

United states buyers please contact for shipping and duties quote

Thanks for looking!

item.title: 2022 G&L CLF Research L1000 Series 750 bass guitar Turquoise leo fender — $1,980
item.description:
$1,980 · Excellent · Bass Monastery

Up for sale is an 2022 all original G&L CLF Research L•1000 Series 750.

In mint condition, weighing 4.56kg. Plays and sounds great, looks stunning. Comes with original hard shell case.

The L•1000 Series 750 is the latest G&L five string bass featuring 19mm string spacing at the bridge, just like Leo Fender’s four-string basses. Series 750 refers to an SAE measurement of “three-quarter-inch” string spacing: 3/4″ = 0.750″ = 19.03mm = nineteen millimeters.

L•1000 Series 750 is the “Widebody Wunkay” built with G&L’s “Wide 5” Saddle Lock bridge plate that houses five of the very same saddles used on G&L four-string basses. That extra space is neatly packed into a curvaceous new body drawing on classic Fender Avenue forms. A new MFD humbucker has been modified for its largest footprint ever, but special care was taken to ensure harmonic fidelity while capturing deep B-string energy.

The “Widebody Wunkay” is a fresh model yet classic Fender Avenue details are everywhere. The graceful early CLF/G&L headstock flows into a slim, rounded neck profile supported by a true dual-action truss rod and twin graphite reinforcement rods. Authentic 1980 cues continue with chunky CLF machined knobs, sweeping control plate, and three-position mini-toggle switch for coil control including the famous L•1000 “OMG” mode.


Shipping & Policies​

Customs Duties: All import duties and taxes are the buyer’s responsibility, except for shipments to the EU or Britain. For shipments to the United States, please contact us first to confirm details related to import fees.​

Final Sale: No returns accepted—review all details and photos, and feel free to message us with questions before purchasing.​

Shipping Options: UPS or FedEx shipping (safe, tracked service for secure transit). For overseas shipment, contact me first to confirm logistics and transit timelines.

However, every player has different preferences regarding action, string height, neck relief, tuning, string gauge, and overall playability. In addition, changes in climate, temperature, and humidity during transport may affect the instrument’s setup. For these reasons, once the bass reaches its new owner, further adjustments and a personalized setup may be required to achieve the desired playing feel and performance. As such adjustments are highly subjective and depend on the individual player, it is not possible to provide a complete custom setup remotely. We recommend that any final setup be carried out by the customer or by a qualified local guitar technician.

item.description:
$5,570.04 · Good · Really Great Guitars

This great piece of Rock n Roll history was owned and played by renowned bass player Greg Masters of 'The Idle Race' band, who were very prominent during the 1960s. This old Gal has been gigged and used on various recordings with the band. Besides The Idle Race, Greg worked with notable artists including Roy Wood, Steve Gibbons and others. Comes with a handwritten letter of provenance & signed by Greg Masters.

This bass is in great original order, with the original Humbucker pickup & baritone switch, and banjo tuners (1959 was the last year of the banjo tuners) She includes playwear you would expect from an instrument of circa 65 years old. Her Mahogany neck & Brazilian Rosewood fingerboard are a joy to feel & play. She sleeps in an original 'Victoria Luggage gold-lined EB-2 Case from the same period. This is a great bass, with that 60s retro "Mudbucker" vibe, but is also a surprisingly versatile bass, particularly due to its functional baritone switch.

item.title: Beautiful and Rare 1969/70 Vox V.G.4. Bass — $1,666.13
item.description:
$1,666.13 · Very Good · Really Great Guitars

Here’s one for the collectors, check out this very cool, all original and indeed very rare 1969/70 Vox V.G.4. This old girl is in fantastic all-round condition. plays amazingly well and has an incredible retro vibe and sound.

The V.G range were likely launched in competition to Gretsch Guitars, the similarities are pretty obvious.

Vox also introduced the Gretsch inspired V.G.6 six string guitar and the V.G.12 twelve string guitar. All three instruments shared the same body with a 17" bout. These models were manufactured for Vox in Japan.

The semi acoustic, double bound Vox V.G.4 featured two single coil pickups sourced from Welson in Italy. The tone of each pickup was shaped by a pair of toggle switches mounted on the upper horn of the instrument. A three position rotary pickup selector switch was mounted to the lower horn. The V.G.4 also featured individual volume for each pickup, a mute switch, painted "f " holes and a bolt on neck. The model number was curiously misstated some of the pick guards. Rather than "V.G.4," the pick guard was incorrectly imprinted "G.V.4" this one actually does have the misprint on the scratchplate.

The 1970 Vox catalog described the V.G.4 as follows: "The bass guitar for the professional with a semi-acoustic, double cutaway body and fast action neck. Fully compensating bridge, tailpiece and adjustable built-in truss rod gives you a full range of string height and length adjustments to suit your style. Two bass frequency single pole pickups have separate tone and volume controls and can be varied by using the pickup selector switch. All fitments are finished in gold, and the guitar is available in green sunburst, transparent red, mahogany and triple sunburst finishes.

$2,836 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Musicman emerged as Leo Fender's second brand and became beloved by bassists worldwide.

Many artists, including Louis Johnson and Flea, are known to use their basses. (Titles omitted)


The Stingray is a classic model that enjoys overwhelming popularity within the brand.

Alongside Fender's Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, it's safe to say it's one of the most popular basses in the world.


This particular instrument is from 1989.

It features a 4-bolt neck joint, a top-load bridge, and a 3-band EQ—specifications unique to that era.


The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.


It's equipped with original humbucking pickups and a 3-band active EQ.


The Stingray's appeal lies in its powerful, punchy low end and crisp attack.


With its 3-band EQ, it allows for a wide range of sound shaping, producing a distinctive sound in various genres, including rock and funk.


The frets have approximately 70% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show overall minor scratches and dents. There are some speckled scratches on the back of the body. A finger rest sticker is attached next to the pickup. Some tarnishing is visible on the metal parts.


The electronics are in good condition.


No obvious modifications were observed.


Controls: Master Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass

Nut Width: 41.5mm

Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm

Scale Length: 34 Inch

Year of Manufacture: 1989

Country of Manufacture: USA

item.title: Sandberg BassTheWorld PJ / Hardcore Reserved Aged Tobacco Sunburst — $3,555.76
item.description:
$3,555.76 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

Geïnspireerd door de Basstheworld TT van Gregor Fris' populaire BassTheWorld YouTube-kanaal. 

Geïnspireerd door de Basstheworld TT van Gregor Fris’ populaire BassTheWorld YouTube-kanaal, is de California VT BassTheWorld zo’n bas die je meteen snapt zodra je ’m vastpakt. Geen poespas, gewoon een strak gebouwde alleskunner met een sound waar je op kunt vertrouwen – of je nu thuis speelt, in de studio zit of het podium op gaat.

De basis begint bij een swamp ash body. Lekker licht, resonant en met precies die punch die je wilt in je laag. De hals is gemaakt van Canadian hard rock maple en voelt stevig en comfortabel aan, met een 34” (864 mm) mensuur en een 14” radius. Dat speelt soepel weg, of je nu lekker groove speelt of wat sneller werk doet. De rosewood toets met witte block inlays geeft ’m die klassieke, herkenbare look.

Qua sound zit je hier helemaal goed. De Kloppmann PJ61 pickups leveren een veelzijdige klank: strak en gedefinieerd in het laag, helder in het midden en met genoeg karakter om overal doorheen te komen. Met alleen volume- en toonregeling houd je het overzichtelijk, maar heb je toch alles in handen om je sound te vormen – van warm en rond tot punchy en direct.

De Sandberg tuners zorgen ervoor dat je stemming gewoon stabiel blijft, ook als je ’m goed gebruikt. Met 22 frets heb je daarnaast genoeg bereik voor zowel traditioneel als moderner spel.

Dit is zo’n bas die niet alleen goed klinkt op video, maar vooral in jouw handen tot leven komt. Geleverd in een gigbag, dus je neemt ’m makkelijk overal mee naartoe. Of je nu funky lijnen speelt, stevige rockpartijen neerlegt of gewoon een solide allround bas zoekt: de California VT BassTheWorld staat z’n mannetje.

item.title: Yamaha SB-55 Bass 1975 Sunburst – Documented MIJ Vintage w/ Original Case & Papers — $2,209.18
item.description:
$2,209.18 · Very Good · Gruuvik Chamber

The Yamaha SB-55 was introduced during one of the most interesting periods in Japanese guitar manufacturing. Built by Nippon Gakki, Yamaha's legendary factory, these basses were designed as original instruments rather than copies of American classics. While many Japanese manufacturers of the era focused on Fender-inspired designs, Yamaha followed its own path, developing a distinctive body shape, pickup design and construction philosophy.

The SB-55 belongs to Yamaha's original SB (Super Bass) series, produced before the later Super Bass 500, SBV and other derivative models appeared. These early instruments have developed a strong reputation for their build quality, unique appearance and warm, articulate tone that stands apart from contemporary Fender-style basses.

According to the original Yamaha warranty card, this bass was purchased new in Japan on March 30, 1975. It remains in remarkably well-preserved condition for a 50-year-old instrument. It comes with its original Yamaha hard case, original documentation, bridge cover and original adjustment tools—an increasingly rare combination.

Fitted with a brand-new set of Pyramid Black Tape Nylon 648/1 strings, this bass produces an exceptionally warm, deep and woody tone with excellent note definition. The black tapes complement the SB-55 exceptionally well, giving it a character that is quite different from most vintage Japanese basses.

The two volume potentiometers have been replaced with quality modern equivalents, and all three control knobs are tasteful vintage-style replacements. Everything else appears to remain original, including the pickups, electronics, bridge, tuners and hardware.

Specifications

  • Year: 1975
  • Made in Japan (Nippon Gakki)
  • Serial Number: 13235
  • Finish: Sunburst
  • Body: Solid Nato
  • Pickups: Yamaha single-coil pickups
  • Weight: 4.3 kg
  • Scale Length: Approximately 34" (864 mm)
  • Nut Width: Approx. 38 mm
  • Neck: Bolt-on Maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood


Condition

Overall, the bass is in very good vintage condition.

The finish shows only honest cosmetic wear consistent with nearly five decades of careful use. The neck is straight, the truss rod functions correctly and the instrument plays comfortably across the fingerboard.

All electronics work as they should.

The original Yamaha hard case remains fully functional and still bears handwritten period markings reading "Muse" and "Blues now", adding a charming piece of its documented history.

Documentation & Accessories

A rare and unusually complete documentation package is included:

  • Original Yamaha warranty card (dated March 30, 1975)
  • Original Yamaha SB-55 owner's manual
  • Original Yamaha hard case
  • Original bridge cover
  • Original Yamaha adjustment tools


Finding a mid-1970s SB-55 with its original paperwork, accessories and hard case is becoming increasingly uncommon.

Notes

Please note that some close-up photographs reveal minor dust particles under strong lighting. These are not finish defects.

Shipping

Ships from the European Union (Estonia). No import duties for EU buyers.

The instrument will be carefully packed to ensure safe transit and shipped via a tracked courier service.

Fully insured and/or express shipping options (DHL, UPS or FedEx) are available upon request.

Minor setup adjustments may be required after transportation and climate adaptation.

Additional Information

Please feel free to message me if you have any questions or would like additional photographs.

More soul-approved vintage Japanese and carefully handpicked basses are available through Gruuvik Chamber — no hype, just the good ones. 👊

item.title: Atelier Z M#265/ CTM 17P / TP-BLUE MH【4.62kg】 — $2,713.20
item.description:
$2,713.20 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!


We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Also, please be sure to read our store policies listed below the instrument description!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――


Since its founding in 1989, Atelier Z has been promoting Japanese-made active basses and continues to be loved by bassists both in Japan and abroad.


The M (MAGNITUDE) series, which has enjoyed enduring popularity since its debut, is one of Atelier Z’s flagship models. Combining a solid, grounded low end with a brilliant, exceptionally clear sound, it is recognized by many musicians as the quintessential active JB bass. Thanks to high-precision craftsmanship, it is set up for low action, making it suitable for any playing style.


This custom model features a striking deep blue finish. Finished in a shade darker than standard, it achieves a look that balances vibrancy with a sense of calm and sophistication.


The sound features an extremely fast attack and exceptional punch when slapping. While maintaining the core of the low end, it clearly reproduces everything from the attack during slapping to the well-defined nuances of fingerstyle playing. This makes it easy to handle across all genres and ensures it won’t get lost in a band ensemble.


It features original pickups and is equipped with the classic Bartolini XTCT preamp. A 2-band EQ, which can also be switched to passive mode, enables a wide range of tone shaping.


Additionally, this custom model features a 17mm string spacing designed with playability in mind. Compared to the standard 19mm spacing, the slightly tighter string spacing helps minimize unnecessary movement during fast phrases and technical playing, improving right-hand control.


A player-oriented instrument that balances the powerful sound characteristic of Atelier Z with ease of use. We highly recommend giving it a try.


[SPEC]

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Maple

Pickup: Atelier Z JBZ-5

Preamp: Bartolini XTCT+Spectrum boost

Controls: 1 Volume, BAL, 1 Tone/Switch (Active ON/OFF), Treble, Bass

Case: Gig Bag

item.description:
$416.88 · Very Good · Dom's Gear Emporium

Want those double bass-esque tones but in a smaller package? Why not try the rich, woody tones of this Tanglewood Sundance TW-55 acoustic fretless?

All solid (sitka spruce top - mahogany back and sides) with an ebony fingerboard, this bass is already strung with RotoSound tru bass black nylon flats to give those sumptuous, smooth and warm vibes. Oe if you prefer, an unused set of Thomastik-Infeld AB 344 roundwound strings is included in the sale. Also included a TGI Extreme gig bag with 20 mm heavy padding in excellent condition.

With the Tanglewood custom B-band bass pre-amp and built in tuner, you have the ability to adjust the tone to suit the gig, whether it be folk, jazz or just a 'stripped-back' session

- Back and sides: Solid African mahogany
- Binding: Wood on body and fingerboard

- Bridge and fingerboard: Rosewood

item.description:

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――


Since its founding in 1989, Atelier Z has been dedicated to popularizing Japanese-made active basses and has continued to be loved by bassists both in Japan and abroad.


This model belongs to the M (MAGNITUDE) series, one of Atelier Z’s flagship lines that has enjoyed enduring popularity since its debut. This series combines a solid, grounded low end with a brilliant, exceptionally clear sound, making it the quintessential active JB bass recognized by countless musicians. Thanks to its high-precision craftsmanship, it is set up for low action, making it suitable for any playing style.


This 70s Limited model features a rear pickup positioned closer to the bridge and a stylish design that omits position marks.

The 70s-style fret layout delivers a crisp, hard-hitting sound, while the absence of position marks allows you to enjoy a straighter, more direct tone with minimal signal loss.

It also features the dynamic slap sound unique to a 4-string bass.


Please take this opportunity to try it out.


-SPEC-

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Maple

Pickup: Atelier Z JBZ-4 (Rear = '70s Position)

Preamp: Bartolini XTCT+Spectrum boost

Controls: 1 Volume, BAL, 1 Tone/Switch (Active ON/OFF), Treble, Bass

Case: Original Gig Case

Weight: 4.12 kg

item.title: 【used】Fender / MIJ Traditional Original 50s Precision Bass BLK Refinish 2021 3.615kg #JD21010990【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,138
item.description:
$1,138 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)

The Precision Bass was the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, instantly captivating bassists worldwide.

Its brilliance remains unchanged and it continues to be loved even more than half a century after its creation.

This model is part of the Made in Japan series, which started under a new system after the closure of Fender Japan.

While maintaining the high quality unique to Japan, it incorporates more specifications and concepts from the American original than ever before.

This model is based on the early specifications called "Original Precision Bass," which predates the current split-coil specification.

The body is made of basswood.

The body has been refinished in black.

The neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

It is equipped with Vintage-Style Single-Coil Precision Bass pickups.

The pickguard has been replaced. This bass boasts a thick, full sound and the crisp attack characteristic of single-coil pickups.

Despite its simple controls, it produces a powerful bass sound that excels in various genres, including rock and blues.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show overall scratches and signs of use. The paint is cloudy overall. There are paint chips of approximately 65.0mm on the body top and 10.0mm on the body back.

The electronics are in good condition.

The body has been refinished. The pickguard has been replaced.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut Width: 42.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 20.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2021
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

SIT Strings, favored by many professional artists, are used during setup.

They are characterized by stable tuning and a powerful, three-dimensional sound. Please enjoy them as well.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Soft Case

Text: Mizuki Ishikawa

Inspection 담당: Hirayama

Product Photos: Tei

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop. *Please note that you will be responsible for shipping and other related expenses.

*Repurchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the purchase price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

*Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by parties other than our company.

* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the text has been altered.

* If the warranty card is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase an instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.

item.description:
$836.55 · Excellent · Sam Cross Bass Player

Beautiful Höfner Club 500/2 bass in black with specific Club bass case and new (used twice) La Bella 760FHB2 Beatle Bass strings.

Small aesthetic only scuffs on the case from the lovely folk at the airport, but all locks and clips are in perfect working order and the case is very well built and tough.

In 2023 I bought the bass for £580 and the case for £150, and in 2026 I bought the strings for £60 (the bass, case and strings all bought from Thomann). This adds up to £790. I will sell it all for £600.

item.title: Ashdown The Saint Soap — $333.23
item.description:
$333.23 · Mint · absno

. UK Sale Only
An online shop's description:
The Ashdown The Saint Soap is part of Ashdown's superb range of 'Roasted' bass guitars. With it's standout offset body, handwound pickup, roasted maple neck and fingerboard, and affordable price point, it's a sure fire winner from the Ashdown team! 
The body of the Saint Soap features a new, albeit already classic looking offset design. Married to the body is a 34" scale roasted maple neck topped with a roast maple fingerboard, delivering great looks whilst also enhancing the tone.

As its name suggests, the bass comes loaded with a Soap Bar pickup - handwound, all-passive and wired through simple master volume and tone controls (with CTS pots). Tonally, you are treated to a rich, organic bass tone with good amounts of punch.

With its standout looks, range of premium appointments and classic, punchy tone, the Ashdown Saint Soap Roasted bass is superb instrument sure to appeal to many bassists!

  • 4-string bass guitar
  • Offset body design
  • Roasted maple neck and fingerboard
  • 38mm nut
  • 34" scale
  • Hand wound soap bar pickup
  • Volume and tone controls

My view:
It is a great looking bass that sounds good too. The construction is excellent and the workmanship and finish are impressive. The finish is called "Ice Blue", but to my eyes it is more like a light sea foam green.

I cannot really comment much about the sounds and playability, which I found to be great, because "to each their own". I like coffee strong and black, you might like it weaker and with milk. I like adding turmeric, ginger, and black pepper; you might not.

I have hardly used this bass because in my infinite stupidity (GAS) I do not have the space to keep it and a certain someone tells me I have too many basses and does not think it goes well in the living room. (She does not like her coffee strong or black!!)

So..... I will have the bass delivered via ParcelForce 24 Hour service, as they are usually more reliable than other services, and it will be securely packaged.

item.title: Sterling Stingray Ray2 Bass Intro Series 2025? 2026? - Electric Blue — $299.76
item.description:
$299.76 · Excellent · absno

I would say mint is a better description, but it is no longer on the dropdown selection.

It still has the plastic on the scratchplate, neckplate and tuners.

I am selling because it was an impulse buy - I used to have a real Stingray or two - but whereas the Ray2 is an excellent bass, it does not have the sounds of the Stingray. Doh! I think that should have been really obvious. So here is a great bass, hardly used, for a really good price.

From the website:
The Intro Series StingRay bass welcomes players new to the Sterling by Music Man family. At the heart of this bass is a passive humbucking bridge pickup for a familiar yet versatile tone. It also features a narrow 40mm nut width, contoured body, and hard maple neck with satin finish for a comfortable, easy playing experience.

item.title: Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series Electric Bass — $292.09
item.description:
$292.09 · Very Good · Guitar Buyer

Thank you for viewing our product selection! ☆


----------------------------------------------------------------------


[Product Description]


- Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series


[Features]


We've just received a PJ-type bass from the Squier by Fender Affinity Series.


This is one of the Affinity Series instruments that combines the classic design inherent to Fender's direct brand with exceptional ease of playability.


The striking red finish paired with a black pickguard creates a simple yet commanding aesthetic that photographs beautifully and commands attention on stage.


This model features a Precision Bass-style split pickup at the front and a Jazz Bass-style single pickup at the rear, making it a practical PJ-type configuration.


In addition to the thick, focused low end characteristic of Precision Basses, blending in the rear pickup adds articulate attack and clarity—a versatile instrument for any working bassist.


With excellent balance between thickness, punch, and tonal definition, it handles rock, pop, punk, and vocalist-fronted band arrangements with equal ease.


The 2-volume, 1-tone control configuration allows intuitive tone shaping from the full-bodied punch of the front pickup to the articulate clarity achieved by blending in the rear.


With straightforward controls but broad sonic range, it's ideal whether you're picking up bass for the first time, looking for a practice instrument, seeking a reliable backup bass, or wanting something affordable for casual gigging.


The Squier by Fender Affinity Series is a popular line that makes genuine Fender-style character accessible and approachable, prized for its user-friendly nature and practical design.


The PJ configuration combines the powerful character of Precision Bass with the cutting clarity of Jazz Bass—a highly versatile format for players seeking wide-ranging tonal possibilities from a single instrument.


With classic looks, approachable tone, and practical PJ specifications, this is an excellent value electric bass.


Whether as a first instrument or as an affordable backup bass, this is an instrument ready to work for you immediately.


The instrument comes without a case—just the body—but this makes it more accessible and should appeal to anyone prioritizing practicality in their search for a bass.


[Specifications]


Product Name: Squier by Fender P-BASS Affinity Series

Type: Electric Bass

Color: Red finish

Strings: 4-string

Controls: 2-control configuration

Pickguard: Black

Fretboard: Rosewood

Position Markers: Dot position markers

Headstock Logo: Squier by Fender P BASS

Headstock Marking: Affinity

Country of Manufacture: Crafted in China

Serial Number: CY13020575

Bridge: 4-saddle type

Tuning Machines: Open gear type

Neck Joint: Bolt-on

Neck Plate: Squier by Fender engraved


[Measurements]

Scale: approx. 868mm

Controls: 2 volume, 1 tone

Weight: approx. 4,065kg

[Condition]


Overall in good shape with minor scratches and dings throughout, and slight adhesive residue visible near the bottom.


Neck: Slightly back-bowed (playability is excellent)

Truss Rod: Good clearance

Frets: approx. 70% remaining

String Height at 12th Fret: 4th string approx. 2.5mm, 1st string approx. 2.0mm

Electronics: No issues


[Service History]


- Full cleaning

- Neck adjustment

- Fret polishing

- Fretboard polishing

- String replacement

- String height adjustment


Playing condition is excellent.


[Included Items]


Instrument only. (Please note: no case is included)


[Storage]


We maintain indoor humidity between 45%–55% and store cased instruments with humidity control packs. While there is always some possibility that an instrument's condition may shift during the period between listing and sale due to the nature of these items, we take every precaution to maintain the condition at time of service as strictly as possible.


----------------------------------------------------------------------


-------------------------------Transaction Information---------------------------------------



[Important Notes]

* Please be sure to read the following.


- If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us via message.


- This is a used item. Please carefully check all photos and the description before purchasing.


- For returns, please refer to the notes below and our shop policy.


- We strive to respond promptly, but please understand that replies or shipping may occasionally be delayed.


[Shipping]

* We ship via EMS, UGX, or FedEx. (For addresses within Japan, we ship via Yamato Transport or Sagawa Express.)


* Depending on your country, we may be unable to ship due to package size restrictions.

We would appreciate it if you could message us to confirm before purchasing.


* Please note that Wednesday is our regular day off.

(No shipments can be made on Wednesdays.)


- Although it varies by country, items usually arrive within about 7-10 days after shipment.


[Packaging]

Regardless of whether the item includes an original box, hard case, or soft case, we pack every item carefully and securely to prevent damage.

(Unpacking may take a little time — thank you for your understanding.)

Shipping insurance is included, so please buy with confidence.


[Please Be Sure to Read]

* As this is a used item, only one is in stock.

* Please note that there may be scratches, dents, or other signs of use that are difficult to see in the photos.

* This item is listed on multiple marketplaces, so there may be a time lag in sales and stock updates.


Due to the above, the item may already be sold at the time of your order.


All of our instruments are play-tested and set up before sale. If an item could not be function-tested, this is stated in the description. All photos are of the actual item, so please refer to them to get an idea of its condition.


As this is a used item, it may show scratches or wear. We cannot accept returns for dents, dings, scratches, or cosmetic wear that are already shown in the photos or noted in the description. Returns are accepted only for defects not mentioned in the description, and only within one week of delivery. If damage during shipping is suspected, confirmation with the carrier and an insurance claim will be required, so please be sure to keep the packaging materials and the item in the condition they arrived in (see our policy).


[Voltage Notice]

The voltage in Japan is 100V, which differs from that of most other countries.

Please check the voltage in your country before purchasing — if it differs, you may need a voltage converter/adapter to use the item in your country.


[Customs Duties]

Depending on your country and the item price, customs duties may be charged in addition to the item price and shipping cost.


We kindly ask that you check the import duties and fees you may incur before purchasing.


* Italy in particular is highly likely to charge additional customs fees, so please be sure to check before purchasing.


If you send us a message, we will do our best to look into it for you as well — please feel free to contact us if you need help.


Thank you very much for your understanding and cooperation.


For details, please see our shop policy.

item.title: 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 Blueburst OHSC Rare Blue Burst Bass — $4,199
item.description:
$4,199 · Very Good · Guitar Chimp

For Sale: 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 Blueburst w/ OHSC | Rare Custom Color

Up for sale is a 2006 Rickenbacker 4003 in the exceptionally rare Blueburst finish, complete with its original Rickenbacker hard-shell case.

Blueburst is one of the most desirable and hardest-to-find custom colors Rickenbacker has ever produced. Available for only a short period, it has become one of the most collectible finishes among modern Rickenbacker enthusiasts. The vibrant Blueburst gloss finish gives this bass incredible visual depth while preserving the unmistakable styling, tone, and feel that have made the 4003 one of the most iconic basses ever built.

This is a 100% original example that has clearly been well cared for over the years.

Please note: The original hard-shell case is included; however, the original paperwork is not included.

Condition

Very Good – 8.5/10

  • Light scratches throughout
  • Some small dings from normal use
  • Minor clear coat bubbling around the lower side of the bridge

The finish bubbling is cosmetic only and is a fairly common occurrence on Rickenbacker basses of this era. It does not affect playability or structural integrity.

Please review all photos carefully for the most accurate representation of condition.

The bass is all original with no cracks, breaks, repairs, or structural issues.

The neck is straight, the original frets are in excellent condition with minimal wear, and the bass has been professionally set up by our in-house luthier. It plays cleanly up and down the neck with no buzzing or dead spots.

The original Rickenbacker hard-shell case is included. The case is in very good condition and one latch has been professionally replaced.

Specifications

  • Model: Rickenbacker 4003
  • Year: 2006
  • Serial Number: 06 02204
  • Finish: Blueburst
  • Body: Solid Maple
  • Neck: Maple Neck-Through Construction
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Radius: 10"
  • Scale Length: 33.25"
  • Frets: 20
  • Inlays: Pearloid Triangles
  • Nut Width: 1.70"
  • Neck Depth: .93" at the 1st Fret
  • Neck Depth: .94" at the 12th Fret
  • Pickups: Original Rickenbacker Hi-Gain Single-Coils
  • Controls: Volume, Volume, Tone, Tone
  • Switching: 3-Way Pickup Selector
  • Outputs: Mono & Ric-O-Sound Stereo
  • Bridge: Original RIC Bridge/Tailpiece
  • Tuners: Schaller Deluxe
  • Weight: 9 lbs 4 oz
  • Case: Original Rickenbacker Hard-Shell Case

Electronics & Features

The 4003 remains one of the most recognizable basses ever produced thanks to its neck-through maple construction, legendary sustain, and unmistakable Rickenbacker tone.

Its pair of Hi-Gain single-coil pickups produce the famous "Rick growl" with tight lows, aggressive mids, and sparkling highs. The Ric-O-Sound stereo output allows each pickup to be routed independently, opening the door to an enormous range of tonal possibilities.

The real highlight, however, is the Blueburst finish. Rarely seen on today's market, it shifts beautifully under different lighting and stands apart from the more common Fireglo, Jetglo, and Mapleglo finishes. It is one of the most collectible modern Rickenbacker colors ever offered.

Tone & Feel

This bass has the unmistakable punch, clarity, and sustain that have defined the Rickenbacker 4003 for decades. Every note rings with exceptional definition, while the maple neck-through construction provides incredible sustain and articulation.

The comfortable medium-profile neck feels substantial without being bulky, making it equally suited for fingerstyle, pick playing, and aggressive rock techniques.

At 9 lbs 4 oz, it has the solid, confidence-inspiring feel players expect from a classic 4003 while remaining well balanced on a strap.
